title: Fixing TF to < 2.16
body:

step_plan.summary: Change the TensorFlow dependency in setup.py extras_require['gpu'] from a <=2.15.0 cap to a <2.16 cap (keeping existing exclusion list) so the package allows all TF 2.15.x patch releases while preventing TF >= 2.16.

steps.operation: Update TensorFlow version spec in gpu extras
steps.reason: Replace the hard cap '<=2.15.0' with a more future-proof '<2.16' so permitted TensorFlow versions include all 2.15.x patch releases while still excluding known-broken versions (keep the existing !=2.9.*, !=2.10.0.* exclusions). This matches the PR intent to allow TF < 2.16 and prevents unnecessarily pinning to a specific patch.
steps.side_effects: Alters package dependency resolution for installations that request the 'gpu' extras: installers may now pick later 2.15.x patches. No code changes are required, but TensorFlow-related unit and regression tests (e.g., tests/regression/test_compatibility_tf.py and tests/unit/recommenders/utils/test_tf_utils.py) must be run to validate compatibility. Downstream consumers or CI configs that pin TF separately (Dockerfiles, CI matrix) may need review to ensure consistency. Packaging metadata change should have no API impact but could change installed TF minor/patch version in environments.
